question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction that is processed by two mirror node importer instances simultaneously during a network partition, causing entityListener.onEntity() to be called twice with the same entity ID and divergent autoRenewAccountId values, resulting in split-brain state where each partition holds a different canonical topic owner?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction such that during a network partition the Range.atLeast(consensusTimestamp) open-ended timestamp range is committed on one partition but not the other, leaving an unbounded topic history range that permanently corrupts topic state queries after partition healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition between the entityListener.onEntity(entity) call and the subsequent createTopic() call inside doUpdateEntity(), causing the Entity row to be persisted without a corresponding Topic row, resulting in a permanently inconsistent database state where the topic entity exists but has no topic metadata?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction that is replayed after a partition heals, causing doUpdateEntity() to call entityListener.onEntity() and entityListener.onTopic() a second time for the same topicId, overwriting a legitimately updated topic state with stale creation-time data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition where the transaction receipt's TopicID returned by recordItem.getTransactionRecord().getReceipt().getTopicID() differs between two partitioned importer instances, causing getEntity() to resolve to different EntityId values and permanently assign the same topic creation event to two different entity IDs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a maximally large adminKey byte payload such that during a network partition the serialized adminKey stored via transactionBody.getAdminKey().toByteArray() causes a memory pressure spike that triggers an OOM condition on one partition node, causing that node to drop the transaction and diverge from the other partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a maximally large feeExemptKeyList (via getFeeExemptKeyListList()) such that the FeeExemptKeyList.newBuilder().addAllKeys().build().toByteArray() serialization produces a payload large enough to cause a partition-induced write timeout on one importer node but not another, creating divergent topic state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with an autoRenewAccount referencing a non-existent account ID, such that during a network partition one importer node resolves EntityId.of(transactionBody.getAutoRenewAccount()) to a valid cached entity while the other does not, causing entity.setAutoRenewAccountId() to store divergent values across the partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a memo field containing a very large string such that entity.setMemo(transactionBody.getMemo()) causes a database write failure on one partitioned node but succeeds on another, resulting in divergent entity memo state that persists after partition healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a negative or zero autoRenewPeriod value such that entity.setAutoRenewPeriod(transactionBody.getAutoRenewPeriod().getSeconds()) stores an invalid period, and during a network partition one node applies this value while another node skips it due to a different validation path, causing divergent auto-renewal behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction that causes consensusUpdateTopicTransactionHandler.updateCustomFee() to be called with a non-empty customFeesList during a network partition, where one partition commits the CustomFee record but the other does not, leaving an orphaned custom fee entry associated with a topic that has no corresponding fee schedule on the other partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit two CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transactions with identical consensusTimestamp values (possible during a partition-induced clock skew) such that both createTopic() calls produce Topic objects with the same Range.atLeast(consensusTimestamp) and topicId, causing a primary key collision or silent overwrite in the topic table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause doUpdateEntity() to execute out of order relative to a subsequent ConsensusUpdateTopic transaction for the same topicId, such that the update is applied before the create, resulting in the create overwriting the update and permanently losing topic configuration changes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a submitKey that is a malformed protobuf byte sequence, such that transactionBody.getSubmitKey().toByteArray() stores corrupt bytes, and during a network partition one importer node stores the corrupt key while another rejects it, causing divergent topic access control state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a feeScheduleKey that is a maximally nested protobuf key structure, such that transactionBody.getFeeScheduleKey().toByteArray() produces a deeply nested byte array that causes stack overflow during deserialization on one partitioned node but not another, creating divergent fee schedule key state?

[File: 'File Name: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction where recordItem.addEntityId(autoRenewAccountId) is called during a network partition, causing the entity transaction tracking to record the autoRenewAccountId on one partition but not the other, resulting in inconsistent entity transaction history that cannot be reconciled after healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition such that the AbstractEntityCrudTransactionHandler.updateEntity() guard check (EntityId.isEmpty(entityId) || !recordItem.isSuccessful()) evaluates differently on two partitioned nodes due to stale entity ID cache state, causing one node to skip entity creation while the other proceeds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with an empty feeExemptKeyList such that the FeeExemptKeyList.newBuilder().addAllKeys(transactionBody.getFeeExemptKeyListList()).build().toByteArray() always produces a non-null byte array (unlike the update handler which stores null for absent lists), and during a partition this semantic inconsistency causes one node to treat the topic as having an explicit empty exemption list while another treats it as having no list, diverging access control?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a custom fee referencing a denominating token ID that does not exist in one partition's entity cache, causing EntityId.of(fixedFee.getDenominatingTokenId()) in updateCustomFee() to resolve to a null or zero EntityId on one partition but a valid ID on another, resulting in divergent custom fee denomination state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a custom fee collector account ID set to the zero account (0.0.0), causing EntityId.of(fixedCustomFee.getFeeCollectorAccountId()) to produce an empty EntityId, and during a network partition one node stores a null collectorAccountId while another stores zero, creating divergent fee collector state that cannot be reconciled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: createTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a consensusTimestamp at Long.MAX_VALUE nanoseconds, causing Range.atLeast(consensusTimestamp) to create a range starting at the maximum long value, and during a network partition this extreme timestamp causes arithmetic overflow in timestamp comparison logic on one node but not another, diverging topic history range state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction during a network partition where the CompositeEntityListener iterates over entityListeners and one listener (e.g., SqlEntityListener) succeeds while another fails mid-iteration, causing partial state where the entity is persisted to SQL but not to a secondary listener, resulting in divergent state across listener backends?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a custom fee amount of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing FixedFee.builder().amount(fixedFee.getAmount()) to store an overflow value, and during a network partition one node stores the overflow amount while another truncates it, creating divergent fee amount state that enables fee bypass after partition healing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction where the receipt contains a TopicID with shard/realm/num values that differ between two partitioned nodes due to partition-induced consensus divergence, causing EntityId.of(recordItem.getTransactionRecord().getReceipt().getTopicID()) to produce different EntityId values and permanently assign the topic to the wrong entity ID on one partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction with a very large number of custom fees in getCustomFeesList(), causing the updateCustomFee() loop to run for an extended period, and during a network partition the long-running loop is interrupted on one node mid-iteration, storing only a partial list of FixedFee objects while the other node stores the complete list?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusCreateTopicTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doUpdateEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSCREATETOPIC transaction that is processed by two mirror node importer instances simultaneously during a network partition, causing entityListener.onEntity() to be called twice with the same entity ID and divergent autoRenewAccountId values, resulting in split-brain state where each partition holds a different canonical topic owner?
